deals_cleared.merger_name: Apax Partners LLP – Sedex Information Exchange Limited
deals_cleared.merger_description: Smith UK Bidco Limited, a special purpose vehicle which is owned by investment funds which are advised by Apax Partners LLP (**Apax**), proposes to acquire 100% of the issued share capital of Sedex Information Exchange Limited (**Sedex**), via a sale and purchase agreement entered into on 6 May 2026 (the **Acquisition**).

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Smith UK Bidco Limited, which is owned by investment funds which
are advised by Apax Partners LLP (Apax) applied for a notification
waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the issued
share capital in Sedex Information Exchange Limited (Sedex), as
described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
26 June 2026

The acquirer, Apax, is a UK limited liability partnership based in
London, UK, and the parent company of a number of entities which
provide investment advisory services to private equity funds
investing in a range of industry sectors (the Apax Funds).
The target, Sedex, is a technology company active in the supply
chain management (SCM) software applications sector, specialising
in data, insights and professional services to empower supply chain
sustainability. Sedex uses a combination of assessment tools,
training and auditing services to provide end-to-end supply chain
solutions that help businesses identify, manage and address social,
ethical, and environmental risks throughout their supply chains –
including through the use of the Sedex Members Ethical Trade Audit
(SMETA), a standardised social audit methodology.
Some Apax Funds’ controlled portfolio companies provide adjacent
services in the broader SCM software applications sector in
Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is a small horizontal overlap between Apax (including
its connected entities and Apax Funds’ controlled portfolio
companies) and Sedex in the supply of SCM software
services, and no overlap in the supply of social audit
services, in Australia
b. there are alternative suppliers of SCM software and social
audit services in Australia
c. there is a low risk of foreclosure or other concerning
conglomerate effects resulting from exclusionary conduct,
bundling or tying of services post-acquisition.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

deals_cleared.merger_description: Brookfield Corporation (**BN**, and together with funds managed and/or advised by BN and/or its affiliates, **Brookfield**), through Windrose France Holdings SAS and related entities, proposes to acquire 100% of the outstanding shares in GB WFC France SAS and certain related interests from Glacis Acquisition S.à r.l. (the **Acquisition**).

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Brookfield Corporation (BN, and together with funds managed
and/or advised by BN and/or its affiliates, Brookfield), through
Windrose France Holdings SAS (Windrose France) and related
entities, proposes to acquire 100% of the outstanding shares in GB
WFC France SAS and certain related interests from Glacis
Acquisition S.à r.l., as described in the transaction documents
provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
Windrose France and Windrose Holdings III (DIFC) Limited applied
for a notification waiver in respect of the Acquisition.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
26 June 2026

The acquirer, BN, is a global investment firm that deploys pools of
discretionary capital globally for investors across its core
businesses, including its asset management business and its
operating businesses focused mainly on renewable power and
transition, infrastructure, private equity, and real estate.
The target, GB WFC France SAS (together with its subsidiaries, WFC)
(a French simplified joint stock company), is the holding company of
the World Freight Company group, that acts as multi-brand air cargo
and freight General Sales Agent, General Sales and Service Agent
and provides outsourced air cargo management services to airlines.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular, there is no overlap between
Brookfield, its portfolio companies and WFC in the supply of air
cargo sales and management services in Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
